title: Build parametric generator engine (spec to SVG to print-ready PDF)
context: The cold-start fraction-tile set (M0) proves one manipulative family can be built manually. M1 replaces hand-crafted generation with a parametric engine: read a manipulative-spec JSON, emit an SVG with physical dimensions locked in mm, convert to a print-ready PDF. The engine must be deterministic and reproducible (same spec + same generator version == same byte-equivalent output across machines) and must not require a headless browser in the core path, so it can run in CI without special environment setup.
objective: Build a TypeScript parametric generator engine that reads a manipulative-spec JSON and produces print-ready PDFs for all paper sizes and colour modes specified in the spec, with the calibration bar embedded automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Reads a manipulative-spec JSON (per math-manip-spec-001 schema) and emits one PDF per (paper-size x colour-mode) combination declared in the spec.
Physical dimensions are locked in mm at the SVG/PDF coordinate level; no runtime scaling is applied.
Embeds a 100 mm calibration bar on every output sheet automatically.
Applies conservative no-full-bleed safe margins automatically for A4 and US Letter.
One spec fans out to A4/Letter x colour/grayscale/ink-saver x standard/large-print variants.
Deterministic and reproducible: same spec + same generator version produces identical output across machines without a headless browser in the core path.
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build and pnpm test.
resources: tasks/math-manip-style-001.json -- style guide v1 (dimensions, tolerance, calibration bar, margins)
tasks/math-manip-design-001.json -- fraction-tile spec as first target
output: A TypeScript generator package (packages/generator or similar) in a pull request, MIT-licensed, with unit tests, a CLI entry point (generate <spec.json> --outdir <dir>), and a worked example for the fraction-tile spec.
